description: Workflow Section — dedicated tab for operational workflows and anti-patterns, separated from regular blog entries

userStories.title: Add workflows tab to blog navigation
userStories.description: As a user, I want a dedicated 'workflows' tab in the blog so operational procedures don't pollute the main feed.
acceptanceCriteria: New tab 'workflows' appears in the view-tabs bar in blog/templates/base.html, between 'feed' and 'chat'
Tab links to /blog/?tab=workflows
Tab shows active state when selected
Regular feed (/blog/?tab=feed) no longer shows entries tagged with 'workflow' or 'anti-pattern'
userStories.notes: Tabs are in blog/templates/base.html line 32-37. Feed filtering is in blog/app.py get_entries() function.

userStories.title: Workflows list view with metadata
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all workflows in a clean list showing trigger, secrets needed, and cost estimate.
acceptanceCriteria: When tab=workflows, the htmx content area shows a list of all entries tagged 'workflow' or 'anti-pattern'
Each workflow card shows: title, date, trigger (from '## Trigger' section), secrets used (from frontmatter 'secrets' field), cost_estimate (from frontmatter)
Anti-patterns have a visual distinction (red/orange badge) vs regular workflows (blue badge)
List is sorted by date descending
Create new Jinja2 partial: blog/templates/partials/workflow_list.html
userStories.notes: Workflow entries have frontmatter fields: tags (contains 'workflow' or 'anti-pattern'), secrets (list), cost_estimate (string). Body has ## sections: Trigger, Passos, Secrets, Armadilhas, Quando funciona, Quando falha.

userStories.title: Workflow detail view with step navigation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click a workflow and see its full content with clear step-by-step navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a workflow card expands it inline (htmx) or navigates to detail view
Detail shows all markdown sections rendered as HTML
Code blocks have copy-to-clipboard button
Sections (Trigger, Passos, Armadilhas, etc.) have anchor-style visual separation
Back button returns to workflow list
Create new htmx endpoint: /htmx/workflow/<slug>
userStories.notes: The blog already renders markdown via the 'markdown' Python package. Reuse existing markdown rendering from feed entries.

userStories.title: Workflow access stats
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see how often each workflow has been consulted so I know which ones are actually used.
acceptanceCriteria: Each time a workflow detail is viewed, increment a counter in blog_fts.db (new table: workflow_views with columns slug, viewed_at)
Workflow list shows view count badge next to each entry
Most-viewed workflows float to top (optional sort toggle: by date or by views)
API endpoint: GET /api/workflow-stats returns JSON with view counts per slug
userStories.notes: blog_fts.db already exists for FTS5 search. Add a simple views table. No authentication needed for stats.

userStories.title: Filter feed to exclude workflows
userStories.description: As a user, I don't want workflows and anti-patterns cluttering the main feed.
acceptanceCriteria: The main feed (tab=feed) filters out entries where tags contain 'workflow' or 'anti-pattern'
The /htmx/entries endpoint respects this filter when tab != 'workflows'
The /api/entries endpoint also respects this filter by default (add ?include_workflows=true to override)
Existing workflow entries still appear in search results
userStories.notes: This is critical — workflows in the feed was the original complaint. Implement alongside US-001.
